explain.rule: Mixed conditionals combine different conditionals when if-clause and result have different time references. Type 1 (past→present): If + past perfect + would + base verb. A past event has present consequences. Type 2 (present→past): If + past simple + would have + pp. A current state means something would have been different in the past.
explain.why_matters: Links past events to present consequences and present states to past results. A sophisticated B2 skill showing mastery of conditional structures.
explain.hebrew_note: Mixed conditionals are a purely English structural challenge. Focus on: which part is about the PAST and which is about NOW? Past→present: past perfect if-clause + would + base verb. Present→past: past simple if-clause + would have + pp.
explain.tip: Ask: which clause is NOW and which is THEN? Past action → present consequence = past perfect + would + base verb (no have!). Current trait → past consequence = past simple (were) + would have + pp.

good_examples.sentence: If I had studied law, I would be a lawyer now.
good_examples.audio_text: If I had studied law, I would be a lawyer now.
good_examples.note: Past event (didn't study law) → present consequence (not a lawyer)

good_examples.sentence: She would be fluent in French now if she hadn't dropped the classes.
good_examples.audio_text: She would be fluent in French now if she hadn't dropped the classes.

good_examples.sentence: If I weren't so disorganised, I would have remembered her birthday.
good_examples.audio_text: If I weren't so disorganised, I would have remembered her birthday.

good_examples.sentence: He would have got the promotion if he were more confident.
good_examples.audio_text: He would have got the promotion if he were more confident.

good_examples.sentence: If we had invested then, we would be very comfortable now.
good_examples.audio_text: If we had invested then, we would be very comfortable now.
good_examples.note: Past missed opportunity → present financial state

good_examples.sentence: I'd be much healthier now if I had quit smoking earlier.
good_examples.audio_text: I'd be much healthier now if I had quit smoking earlier.

good_examples.sentence: If she spoke better English, she would have got the job abroad.
good_examples.audio_text: If she spoke better English, she would have got the job abroad.

good_examples.sentence: If he weren't so impatient, he would have waited for a better offer.
good_examples.audio_text: If he weren't so impatient, he would have waited for a better offer.

good_examples.sentence: She would understand the system better now if she had done the training.
good_examples.audio_text: She would understand the system better now if she had done the training.
good_examples.note: Past skipped step → present knowledge gap

good_examples.sentence: If I hadn't moved to Tel Aviv, I would be living in Haifa now.
good_examples.audio_text: If I hadn't moved to Tel Aviv, I would be living in Haifa now.

bad_examples.wrong: If I had studied law, I would have been a lawyer now.
bad_examples.correct: If I had studied law, I would be a lawyer now.
bad_examples.reason: Result is NOW → use would be (not would have been).

bad_examples.wrong: If I wasn't so shy, I would have talked to her.
bad_examples.correct: If I weren't so shy, I would have talked to her.
bad_examples.reason: Second conditional with be → use were (not was) for all subjects.

bad_examples.wrong: If she would have stayed, she would be here now.
bad_examples.correct: If she had stayed, she would be here now.
bad_examples.reason: No would have in the if-clause. Past perfect: if she had stayed.

bad_examples.wrong: She would been a doctor now if she had studied medicine.
bad_examples.correct: She would be a doctor now if she had studied medicine.
bad_examples.reason: Result: would + base verb (be). Not would been.

bad_examples.wrong: If I have more confidence, I would have got the promotion.
bad_examples.correct: If I were more confident, I would have got the promotion.
bad_examples.reason: Current trait → past simple (were) in if-clause for mixed type 2.

exercises.question: If I ___ (study) medicine, I ___ (be) a doctor now.
exercises.hint: Past event → present consequence. Past perfect + would + base verb.
exercises.explanation: had studied (past perfect) + would be (present consequence).

exercises.question: She ___ (be) fluent now if she ___ (not/drop) the lessons.
exercises.explanation: would be (now) + hadn't dropped (past perfect).

exercises.question: If I ___ (not/be) so disorganised, I ___ (remember) her birthday.
exercises.explanation: weren't (present) + would have remembered (past).

exercises.question: He ___ (get) the promotion if he ___ (be) more confident.
exercises.explanation: would have got (past) + if he were (present trait).

exercises.question: If we ___ (invest) ten years ago, we ___ (be) comfortable now.
exercises.explanation: had invested + would be (now).

exercises.question: I ___ (be) much healthier now if I ___ (quit) smoking earlier.
exercises.explanation: would be healthier now + if I had quit.

exercises.question: If she ___ (speak) better English, she ___ (get) the job abroad.
exercises.explanation: spoke (present simple) + would have got (past).

exercises.question: If he ___ (not/be) so impatient, he ___ (wait) for a better offer.
exercises.explanation: weren't + would have waited.

exercises.question: If I ___ (take) that offer, I ___ (live) in Paris now.
exercises.explanation: had taken + would be living (now).

exercises.question: She ___ (understand) better now if she ___ (do) the training.
exercises.explanation: would understand (now) + if she had done (past).

exercises.question: If I had saved more money, I ___ be able to retire now.
exercises.explanation: would be able — present consequence.

exercises.question: If she ___ more patient, she would have been a better teacher.
exercises.explanation: were — present trait in mixed type 2.
exercises.distractors: had been / would be / was being

exercises.question: He ___ be living in New York now if he had accepted the transfer.
exercises.explanation: would be living = present consequence.

exercises.question: If I weren't so afraid of flying, I ___ have visited them years ago.
exercises.explanation: would have visited = past result of present state.

exercises.question: Which is a mixed conditional?
exercises.answer: If she had studied harder, she would be a doctor now.
exercises.explanation: Past perfect + present result = mixed conditional.
exercises.distractors: If she studies harder, she will become. / If she studied harder, she would become. / If she had studied, she would have become.

exercises.question: If I ___ more disciplined, I would have finished the project on time.
exercises.explanation: were — current characteristic in mixed type 2.

exercises.question: She ___ fluent in Spanish now if she had kept practising.
exercises.explanation: would be — present consequence.
exercises.distractors: would have been / had been / was

exercises.question: If I knew him better, I ___ have asked for help.
exercises.explanation: would have asked — past result of present limitation.

exercises.question: Find and fix: 'If I had studied law, I would have been a lawyer now.'
exercises.answer: If I had studied law, I would be a lawyer now.
exercises.hint: Result is NOW — use would be
exercises.explanation: Present consequence → would be. Not would have been.

exercises.question: Find and fix: 'If she would have stayed, she would be here now.'
exercises.answer: If she had stayed, she would be here now.
exercises.hint: No would have in the if-clause
exercises.explanation: if she had stayed (past perfect).

exercises.question: Find and fix: 'She would been a doctor now if she had studied medicine.'
exercises.answer: She would be a doctor now if she had studied medicine.
exercises.hint: would + base verb (be) — not would been
exercises.explanation: would be — base verb after would.

exercises.question: Put the words in the correct order:
exercises.answer: If I had taken that job, I would be living in London now.
exercises.explanation: had taken + would be living now.

exercises.question: Put the words in the correct order:
exercises.answer: If she weren't so shy, she would have spoken to him.
exercises.explanation: weren't + would have spoken.

exercises.question: Put the words in the correct order:
exercises.answer: If we had invested then, we'd be rich now.
exercises.explanation: had invested + we'd be = we would be (now).

I would probably be living somewhere very different now.

if she hadn't had such a difficult start.

speak.sentence: If we had started earlier, we wouldn't be in this situation now.

if I hadn't given up the lessons.

she would have tried much sooner.
